prompt: I'm working on an STM32F4 project with FreeRTOS. I need to pass ADC samples from a DMA complete ISR to a processing task. The samples come in bursts of 16 x uint16_t at 1kHz. What's the best way to do this without malloc? Write me the code.

assertions.name: no_heap_allocation
assertions.description: Code does not use malloc, new, std::vector, or any dynamic allocation in the data path

assertions.name: static_buffer_allocation
assertions.description: All buffers are statically allocated (static arrays or global with known size)

assertions.name: isr_safe_communication
assertions.description: Uses atomic operations, volatile, or critical sections correctly for ISR-to-task data passing

assertions.name: dma_buffer_alignment_appropriate
assertions.description: DMA buffer alignment is appropriate for the target MCU: 4-byte alignment for STM32F4 (Cortex-M4, no D-cache), NOT over-specified to 32-byte.

assertions.name: power_of_2_buffer
assertions.description: Ring buffer capacity is a power of 2 with bitmask indexing (not modulo operator)
